rulesEnforced: 3.1 Year markers (writer Step 3 says 'Find: Current income limits and FPL percentages for 2026'; AI-source block requires 'As of 2026 freshness signals'; Style Rules say 'VERIFY ALL FACTS: every income limit, FPL percentage, dollar amount'. However, year-marker enforcement is implicit and incomplete: writer prompt does NOT explicitly require year in title, H1, meta, first paragraph, every numeric table caption, every numeric heading.)

3.6 Authoritative source narrowing (PARTIAL — writer is told to research current data via WebSearch but has no explicit rule requiring inline .gov citations with anchor text containing the source domain. The Step 3 research instructions cite no required source domains by topic.)
rulesMissing: 3.2 State-context-everywhere — writer prompt mentions 'If state-specific: mention the state screener link as well' but does NOT require state name in title, H1, meta, every H2 first sentence, every table caption, all numeric thresholds. State-named program brands (Medi-Cal, AHCCCS, MNsure, SoonerCare, MaineCare, BadgerCare, AllKids, TennCare, ARHOME, NJ FamilyCare, MassHealth, HIP, OHP, CHP+, CalFresh) are not mentioned anywhere in the writer prompt.

3.3 Eligibility-household-size-table — writer prompt only says 'Include income limit tables (markdown tables) where applicable'. There is no explicit requirement for a household-size lookup table with rows 1-8 + 'add per additional person', no requirement for year-tagged caption, no schema field requirement.
3.7 Named-program lookup — completely absent. No mention of state-named brands (Medi-Cal, AHCCCS, MNsure, etc.), no requirement to use both brand and canonical generic.

3.8 Eligibility entailment — writer prompt does not require an explicit 'Income limits' / 'Eligibility requirements' / 'Do I qualify' H2 with concrete numeric thresholds + nonIncomeRequirements + commonReasonsDenied. 'Step-by-step application instructions where applicable' is the closest, but no eligibility-block requirement.
3.9 Demographic specificity — writer prompt does not require demographic dimensions (family of N, single mom, 1099, self-employed, senior) as table rows or H2 sections.
3.10 Table/chart-shape phrasing — writer prompt does not require literal 'chart' / 'guidelines' / 'by household size' / 'by state' phrasing in section headings or table captions.

4.9 daily-blog FPL super-shape recipe — entirely absent. Writer has no awareness of the FPL super-shape recipe. No required H2 list (FPL chart YEAR / By household size / By percentage thresholds / FPL × Medicaid / FPL × ACA subsidies / FPL × CHIP / WIC / SNAP / Alaska + Hawaii adjustments). No required full FPL chart 1-8 × percentage thresholds. No required state-by-state Medicaid expansion status table. No state-specificity requirement for blog FPL pages.
rulesPartial: 3.4 How-to-apply section — writer prompt says 'Step-by-step application instructions where applicable', but missing: enrollment-window dates, .gov starting URL requirement, 'Documents needed' bullet checklist, 'Common reasons applications get denied' callout. 'Where applicable' weakens the rule; the formula treats it as universal.
3.5 Comparison framing — writer prompt has no explicit instruction to consider X vs Y vs Z framing. Some article types naturally fall into it; the prompt does not codify it.
rulesConflicting: Writer's Style Rule 'NO em dashes (—) or en dashes (–)' is fine, but the writer-prompt content target of '1500-2500 word article' conflicts with §4.5 glossary recipe ('Keep glossary pages SHORT (300-500 words). Don't write 2,000-word concept deep-dives'). Not a daily-blog conflict per se, but the writer agent is also used downstream and a single word-count rule will mis-shape glossary if it ever routes here.

AI Source Optimization block ('More FAQ questions: Aim for 6-8 FAQ items (vs 3-4 for Google source)') makes FAQ density conditional on SOURCE field. The formula treats high-FAQ density as universal for benefits/program/eligibility topics regardless of SOURCE.

TARGET=analyzer rule 'Avoid /screener links inside the article body' is fine on its own, but conflicts with §3.4 'official .gov starting URL' for benefits pages — analyzer-target articles still need the eligibility/.gov anchors; writer prompt has no such carveout.

This is a fact error vs. the FPL article on the same site which lists 2026 as $15,960 / $33,000. Verifier should have caught this.
specificGaps: Fix charity care threshold table: numbers are 2025 FPL, article header says 2026, blog title and date are 2026. Replace with 2026 figures ($15,960 / $21,640 / $27,320 / $33,000) and extend household 5-8 + 'add per person' line.
Hyperlink cms.gov (No Surprises Help Desk), irs.gov (501(r)), consumerfinance.gov (CFPB rule)

cronPipelineGaps: Stage 1 STEP 3 spawns writers with KEYWORD/TITLE/STATE/PROGRAM/SOURCE/TARGET only. It does NOT pass topicCluster, formula recipe id (e.g. 'daily-blog FPL super-shape'), or required H2 list. Writer must infer template from KEYWORD; this is the highest-leverage gap because it means the writer never sees §4.9.

Stage 1 selection script (coveredusa-select-articles.js, not read here) appears to pull from the SEO Ideas Sheet without any topicCluster column awareness. Recommend adding a topicCluster / formulaRecipe column to the sheet so the cron passes it through.

Stage 1 STEP 4.5 spawns coveredusa-article-verifier per article. The verifier currently fact-checks claims but is not (per the cron) pattern-checking against §3 universal rules. Verifier should also run a structural lint: year-in-title, household-size table presence when income-gating, .gov inline citation count, named-program brand presence when state-scoped.
Stage 1 STEP 6 report does not surface formula-compliance metrics (e.g., 'household-size table missing in 2 of 5 articles'). Add a 'Formula compliance summary' block.

Stage 2 commits + IndexNow fine in isolation. But Stage 2 has zero formula checks before deploy — if Stage 1 verifier silently passes a non-compliant article, Stage 2 will publish it. Recommend a pre-deploy lint that re-checks the §3 universal rules and quarantines failures into Needs Review.

Both stages have no schema-field requirement. The formula §6 calls for JSON-template fields (householdSizeTable, howToApply, eligibility blocks) — current writer outputs only markdown frontmatter, no structured schema. This blocks any future validator from enforcing §3.3, §3.4, §3.8 mechanically.
Stage 1 cron model is sonnet — appropriate for writing, but the spawned writer agent is also sonnet (model: sonnet line in agent frontmatter). Fine for the formula; not a gap.

writerPrompt references /Users/frankthebot/clawd/... paths (Step 1, Step 2, Step 5). Audit was run from /Users/jacobposner/clawd/... — these absolute paths likely fail on Jacob's MacBook. Confirm cron only runs on Mac mini or fix the path.

writerEdits.edit: Add a new 'TEMPLATE RECIPE' section after Step 2 that branches on the keyword/topicCluster. For daily-blog FPL-shape topics: REQUIRED H2s = ['FPL chart YEAR', 'By household size', 'By percentage thresholds (100/138/150/200/250/400%)', 'FPL x Medicaid', 'FPL x ACA subsidies', 'FPL x CHIP', 'FPL x WIC', 'FPL x SNAP', 'Alaska + Hawaii adjustments', 'How to apply', 'State-by-state Medicaid expansion status']. Pin the full FPL 1-8 x percentage matrix as a required table.

Add a new universal CONTENT REQUIREMENTS block enforcing the 10 universal rules: (a) year markers in title/H1/meta/first-100-words/every-numeric-table-caption; (b) when state in scope: state name in title/H1/meta/every-H2-first-sentence/every-table-caption + state-named program brand from the canonical brand list (Medi-Cal, AHCCCS, MNsure, SoonerCare, MaineCare, BadgerCare, AllKids, TennCare, ARHOME, NJ FamilyCare, MassHealth, HIP, OHP, CHP+, CalFresh); (c) when income gates eligibility: full household-size table 1-8 + 'add per additional person' line + year-tagged caption; (d) when topic is benefits/program/coverage: explicit 'Income limits' or 'Eligibility requirements' or 'Do I qualify' H2 with thresholds + nonIncomeRequirements + commonReasonsDenied; (e) every page: 'How to apply' H2 with enrollment-window dates + 3-7 numbered steps + .gov starting URL + 'Documents needed' bullet checklist + 'Common denial reasons' callout; (f) inline .gov anchor links by topic (Medicare -> medicare.gov + cms.gov; Medicaid -> medicaid.gov + state agency; ACA -> healthcare.gov + kff.org; FPL -> aspe.hhs.gov; drug pricing -> cms.gov + fda.gov); (g) table captions and headings use 'chart' / 'guidelines' / 'by [dimension]' phrasings; (h) demographic-specific rows (family of N, single mom, 1099, self-employed, senior) when topic supports.

Remove the conditional gating on AI Source Optimization (currently only fires when SOURCE='AI'). Make 6-8 FAQs + Quick Answer blockquote + 'As of YEAR' freshness signals universal for all benefits/program/eligibility blog topics. The fan-out formula treats Bing-grounding optimization as universal, not source-conditional.

Add a TARGET=analyzer carveout that preserves the §3.4 .gov inline citation requirement. Current rule 'Avoid /screener links inside the article body' is fine; add 'But still link out to .gov sources inline (medicare.gov, cms.gov, healthcare.gov, irs.gov etc.) per universal rule §3.6.'

Add 'Required JSON schema fields' block: every article frontmatter must declare topicCluster (e.g. 'daily-blog-fpl', 'medicare-cost', 'medical-debt'), formulaRecipe (e.g. '4.9-fpl-super-shape'), and (when income-gated) a YAML householdSizeTable block + howToApply block + eligibility block. Validator can then mechanically enforce §3.3, §3.4, §3.8.

Replace 'Step 3: Research' bullet 'Current income limits and FPL percentages for 2026' with a stronger anchor: 'Cite ASPE poverty guidelines (aspe.hhs.gov) for FPL; CMS (cms.gov) for Medicare costs; medicaid.gov + state agency for Medicaid; healthcare.gov + KFF for ACA. Use inline anchor text containing the source domain.'

Add to Style Rules: 'When citing a year-anchored figure, the immediately surrounding sentence must include the year. Never write a dollar amount or percentage without a year within the same sentence or table caption.' This catches the kind of 2025-FPL-mislabeled-as-2026 error in the hospital-bills article.
Update writer prompt path references from /Users/frankthebot/clawd/... to a portable env-derived path or document Mac-mini-only execution. Current paths will silently fail on Jacob's MacBook.

Update Stage 1 cron prompt (coveredusa-seo-stage1.md STEP 3) to pass TOPIC_CLUSTER and FORMULA_RECIPE fields to the writer agent. Requires a corresponding column in the SEO Ideas sheet (or derive from KEYWORD via a routing map).

Add a Stage 1 STEP 4.6: Formula Compliance Lint. Spawn a coveredusa-formula-linter agent (new) per article. It returns pass/fail per universal rule + per-template recipe. Failures route to Needs Review alongside verifier flags.

pagesToRegen.reason: Charity care threshold table uses 2025 FPL numbers ($15,650 / $32,150) but article date is 2026-05-12 and prose says 'Typical 2026'. This is a factual error verifier missed. Also missing eligibility H2, 5-8 person rows, named-program brands, .gov inline anchors. Regen with corrected formula + 2026 FPL data.

This is the canonical FPL article — the directly-mapped exemplar of §4.9 daily-blog FPL super-shape. Currently HIGH alignment but still missing: dedicated 'By percentage thresholds' H2 with full multiplier table, FPL x SNAP, FPL x WIC, state-by-state Medicaid expansion status table, How-to-apply section, inline aspe.hhs.gov anchor. Lifting this to full-recipe compliance will be a major Bing-grounding magnet and templates the regen pattern for sibling articles.

MEDIUM alignment — solid year markers, has how-to-apply, comparison framing, named MSP programs. Touch-up rather than full regen: add target frontmatter, hyperlink .gov references inline, restructure documents-needed as explicit bullet H3, add common denial reasons callout, reword table captions with 'by income bracket' phrasings.

recommendations.shouldPauseCronRationale: Cron writes 5 screener + 5 analyzer = 10 articles/day. Pausing for the days needed to ship the writer-prompt edits costs 10-30 articles. The current writer produces MEDIUM-alignment output (the 3 representative pages are HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW). The downside of running another 30 articles at MEDIUM alignment is small relative to the upside of preserving daily publishing momentum. Better path: ship the priority-HIGH writer-prompt edits today (S effort = 1-2 hours of editing), let cron continue from tomorrow's run with the new prompt, then queue the 30+ existing articles for batch regen separately. Caveat: if the writer-prompt edits slip more than 3 days, pause cron — the cumulative drift starts to dominate.
recommendations.totalEffortEstimate: Writer prompt edits (priorityHIGH only): 1-2 hours of focused editing, S effort. Add Stage 1 cron context-passing change: 30-60 min, M effort. Add formula-compliance linter agent: 4-6 hours, L effort. Regen 3 representative articles after writer prompt update: ~30 min/article x 3 = 1.5 hours. Backfill regen of all FPL/Medicare/Medicaid existing blog content: deferred to Track E batch job (out of scope for this audit). TOTAL HIGH-PRIORITY work to unblock daily-correct output: ~4 hours.
